Boutros take on falafel was interesting but nothing to write home about. At the end of the day there were some real stand out dishes here, but also some clear misses as well. Fantastic food and experience! The lamb shanks were incredibly tender and full of flavorone of the best Ive had. The mushrooms hummus was rich, creamy, and absolutely delicious. Every dish was beautifully prepared and served hot and fresh. The service was friendly and attentive, and the atmosphere felt warm and welcoming. I left completely satisfied and already excited to come back. Highly recommend. Amazing Lebanese food in this cozy restaurant. We tried half the items on the menu and they were all exquisite. What stood out: the hummus with mushrooms, and the lamb shank (which is enough food for 4 if you order appetizers). Good wine selection too. The negative: just after our third course was served, our server informed us we have a reservation at 8:20 on this table (which was in 20 minutes; we had placed our drinks order at 6:45 so we thought this was tooooo short). So when you book, confirm that youre not going to get kicked out. Fantastic Middle Eastern restaurant on Atlantic Avenue in Brooklyn. I'm not usually a big fan of small plate spots but this was an exception. The dishes were fresh and dripping in flavor; definitely try the Short Rib Shawarma Hummus and the Duck Fat Batata Harra Adding this to my list of favorite Lebanese spots. Very creative take on traditional Lebanese cuisine - great flavors. The staff were very welcoming and kind, we even got to meet the owners. Everything was amazing but I have to say my favorite thing was the orange blossom ice cream *chefs kiss*

reviews_text_combined: Such a fantastic spot for bagels and so much more! They have an incredible variety of spreads to choose from, plus pancakes, blintzes, salads, wraps, sandwiches, and desserts. Highly recommend! Absolutely spectacular Bagel place on the UWS. We tried Modern, we tried Tal, we tried Absolute Bagels (now closed) and were happy that we found this amazing place, but sad that we did not find this sooner! Self-service options, extensive selection, quick service and most importantly - reasonably priced (Modern has good selection, but expensive and is closed on Sat. and Tal is just expensive). This will be our go-to place for bagels. Broadway Bagels food is delicious! Freshly prepared with high quality ingredients & and incredibly diverse menu. Their customer service is excellent, from the folks who take your order to the ones who deliver it (piping hot!). A big shout out to the staff who definitely keep us well fed, especially during one of the most challenging times in recent history, the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. High on my UWS bagels list! The service is great. Theyll customize anything you want (in my case, a BLT with light mayo on a plain bagel). Most bagel shops will do that, but in this case, its not sloppy and doesnt feel random. They know how to customize right the first time. My bagel was also really well wrapped which goes a long way for me. Every neighborhood deserves a dependable bagel shop, and for me, Broadway Bagels plays that role beautifully. Their bagels have that perfect balancechewy yet flavorful, hearty without tipping into dense brick territory. My go-to order is the everything bagel with green onion cream cheese, a combination that consistently earns its place as a star performer in the bagel world. Now, lets address the elephant in the roomor rather, the mountain: the cream cheese. Yes, theyre generous to the point of excess. Its practically an architectural feat. I usually end up editing the portion myself, but on the bright side, it makes the bagel hearty enough to stretch into two meals. Efficiency and indulgence in one. The one drawback is the absence of indoor seating. This isnt a linger with your laptop type of spot. Instead, I take my bagel three blocks down to Central Park, which, truthfully, turns the whole experience into something rather idyllic. Bagel in hand, city all around, nature just aheadhard to complain. A neighborhood classic, and one Im grateful to have nearby.

reviews_text_combined: Ive been a gourmet grocery store enthusiast for a few months now. The smoothies here are okay. Ive had worse smoothies, but Ive also had much better smoothies. They have all sorts of other desserts, meals, prepped food that looks super good. Good selection of gluten free and vegan options. On this episode of finding the best grocery store rotisserie chicken, we have Butterfield Market in the Upper East Side. Its the land of Alo Sweats, Aritzia Puffers, and $10 rice and peas. Their whole vibe is trying to be like the Erewhon of NY. I must note, there is an asterisk on this review. They only had a cold rotisserie chicken that ran $20.99. When I asked the guy at the deli counter if they made any hot tissis, he said no but he could heat it up and it would still be good. As annoyed as I was at the pricepoint of $20.99, I respect the hell out of them for wanting smoke. Let the record show, Butterfield is ducking NO smoke. One cant help but wonder why they only sell a cold rotisserie chicken in the first place. What is the best use case for that? Kind of hurts my head Nonetheless, I got the heated up chicken and took it over to central park to rip the deceased bird limb from limb. Things started off well with the breast, as I felt it was going to land in the low 7 range. I was shocked how decent it was for it being heated up. But then, the leg came a long. It was essentially inedible. Plywood dry. Sahara Desert dry. The dryness from the reheat reared its ugly head. I winced right as I bit into it, simultaneously reaching for the $5 Voss water I had just bought. Final score was a 5.3/10. Terrible. But again, respect to Butterfield for stepping up to the plate. I also don't know if this really counts. I would absolutely come back to re-rate if they start selling a hot bird. IG @jnov_ Tiktok @jnov__

The service was amazing, every single thing we ordered was delicious (cocktails, coffee, appetizers, savory, sweet), and the vibe was so fresh and French. Listen, I'm tearing up just thinking about it. Wait in line. Enjoy your petitel djeuner. It's worth it.

The Highlights: The Food: Traditional Georgian flavors that feel both adventurous and comforting. Every dish was a conversation starter. The Service: Truly top-tier. Our server was originally from Georgia and treated us like guests in their own home. They were incredibly patient, helping us navigate the menu and giving us "the whole 9 yards" on the proper way to eat the traditional dishes. The Drinks & Vibe: Relaxed yet polished. The environment is pleasant, the drinks are great, and its a fantastic spot for groups. Don't Skip Dessert: You have to order the Napoleon cake. It is quite literally to die forit has the perfect amount of crunch and flavor without being overly sweet. It was the highlight of the night! Verdict: 5/5 stars. Great food, even better service. Highly recommended for team dinners or anyone wanting a delicious "culinary trip" to Tbilisi without leaving Manhattan. We absolutely loved Chama Mama! First time having Georgian food. So much to love. Flavorful food, honest craftsmanship, stunning drinks, incredibly friendly staff, beautiful design of the space. While I've seen and understand criticisms of the steep pricepoint for "homey" food, the investment in a meal at Chama Mama is worth the authentic experience, and most of all the DELECTABLE food. Stars of the show were the Beef Kharcho Soup (delicious tomato flavor, hint of spice, stewed beef, wheat), the Khinkali (excellently spiced beef & pork in our case, bathing in a wonderful meat broth inside the dumpling wrapper), and the Khachapuri (cheesy, delicious bread, a must-try food experience), and don't sleep on the mocktails and lemonades. Fantastic restaurant, from the food all the way to the branding. Our server, she was very kind and professional, wine tastings, cocktails, were all above expectations and quite delicious. The food though? Excellent, phenomenal, superb! We enjoyed our meal quite a bit. Easily a place wed come back to. Georgian food is a must try, and Chama Mama does it justice! This was our second visit, and it was just as amazing as the first! We ordered the Adjaruli khachapuri, chicken mtsvadi, honey cake ice cream, and chocolate cloud cake, along with the drinks (passionfruit caramel lemonade and Chteau Buera Rkatsiteli). Every single item was absolutely delicious. The service was also top-notch! Truly outstanding. Easily a 10 out of 10. We cant wait to come back! Had an awesome time at Chama mama. Food was awesome, showed up without a resi and was seated at the bar which was comfy. Bartender was great and the food was even better. Definitely get the khachipuri and khinkali. The ricotta pancakes were also incredible. Overall a great spot with cosy vibes and great service.

Last night my fianc and I headed to a show at the St. George Theatre in Staten Island and decided to take a stroll around the neighborhood to find a spot for dinner. We came across Chang Noi Thai and decided to give it a try and were so glad we did! From the moment we walked in, the bartender and our server were incredibly kind and welcoming. I tried to order on line for a pad Thai with chicken (no extra chicken which is a thing now apparently), and a basil chicken (no extra chicken), plus driver fee, plus taxes, plus tip was about $60!!! reviews_text_combined: We arrived at 1pm on a Saturday. There was a 1-2 hour wait. We decided to sit in the front desk area and review the menu. He was an incredible waiter from start to finish kind, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. He took the time to explain how hot pot works and made great menu recommendations that were spot on. His knowledge, patience, and positive energy made the entire experience smooth and enjoyable. Shawn has a rare ability to make you feel completely comfortable while also delivering top-tier service. Truly the best waiter Ive ever had. He made the night memorable, and Ill absolutely be back because of him. The place is famous of long waiting line for a good reason, it is best hot pot spot in New York City, tastes real authentic as you were in China. They offer a good selection of items on their menu, all fresh and high quality, which is important for hot pot. Few different types of soup base, even you don't eat spicy you can choose mushroom or herb one, both were tasty and good. My favorite dish is the beef with milk, it's fun to watch the waitress preparing it and the meat so tender and fresh after putting into the hot pot. This place is my No.1 choice when it comes to hot pot. Decent but slightly pricey hotpot place with lots of unique items on the menu. Everything was fresh quality and tasted good, except for the Milky Beef (6/10). They pour milk to marinate the beef, which supposedly makes it more tender, but I didnt think it was that special. What I really liked though was the Fresh Bamboo Shoot (10/10) because of the distinct texture. You dont find this on the menu of a lot of hotpot places so it felt particularly special. The wait can be long but you can add yourself to their online waitlist. Also they charge per person for dipping sauce, which is why I docked one star off. Its not uncommon for hotpot places that are not AYCE, but I still dont like it. Lao Zao Chongqing Hot Pot has this beautiful store front. As you enter the wooden doors, you are transported to a traditional village setting of stone and bamboo architecture, hanging lanterns and a water-wheel that separates the higher floor from the lower floor. Even before you sit at your table to savour the food offering, the setting of the restaurant really sets the mood. Service was very efficient and super friendly. The staff were very helpful and provided wonderful recommendations and suggested portions. We opted for the Triple Flavor Pot which consisted of a mushroom soup base, a tomato soup base and mild beef broth soup base. The beef broth was my favourite but a note of caution, the mild is fairly spicy especially as the dried chilies continue to infuse in your pot. Would recommend taking out the dried chilies if it gets too spicy. The complimentary starter of spicy pickled vegetables was also amazing so much so that we asked for extra. Some of the standout dishes that we ordered; Milky Beef - when severed, the milk would be poured over the beef and this supposedly helped tenderise the meat. Either way, the beef was delicious, tender and succulent. The Shrimp Paste was served in a hollowed out bamboo, in a crushed ice bucket with its own unique serving spoon. This was fresh and tasty and definitely worth trying. The Stir-Fried Bean Curd Rolls was another favourite. Cooking time for this was a few seconds as it would soak up the soup and inflate very quickly when cooked. We also tried the Thick Sliced Tender Beef, Marbled Beef and Beef Ball which were all amazing. Definitely great to go with a group so you can try the variety of meats, seafoods and vegetables.

Update October 2025: We almost go every weekend or every other weekend. This time I tried their frozen yogurt. As a frozen yogurt fanatic, we did not like it. Original Review- September 2025: Corto Brooklyn is one of my favorite coffee spots. The Severini focaccia with a cappuccino (I always ask for a bit of honey) is my go-to combo so delicious. Its about $25 per person for coffee and a sandwich. It does get busy, but its absolutely worth the wait. I especially love sitting outside on the sidewalk terrace, it makes the experience even better. A true neighborhood gem. The cashier with the blue eyes is so lovely, just the most wonderful service! The sandwiches are fantastic but meat-heavy. And specifically pork-heavy. I wish they had more veg or tuna or chicken options. The bundt cake was really good and the ambience is nice. Comfortable and not stuffy. Among our clients on Supp, Corto is probably one of my favorites, and not just because I am Italian lol The vibes are super chill. This is an awesome spot to have a small bite, do some remote work, and, most importantly, get your mid-afternoon caffeine boost with a nice, good espresso. They also have a small garden in the back with more seating and a wine bar that I haven't had the pleasure of trying yet. I have to come back on a Friday afternoon, so I don't feel guilty about having a drink while working. Ciao a presto :) This review is about sustainability only! By 2050 there will be more plastic in the ocean than fish. Over 95% of the plastic that we produce and consume does not actually get recycled, but rather ends up in landfills, incinerators, in our oceans, and in our bodies as microplastics. While I am happy to see that your shop offers for-here, reusable mugs and plate ware and cutlery to customers, it seems that no one knows to ask for them. Not one person in your coffee shop the other day was drinking out of a multi-use, real cup. Instead, those who were there to work, meet a friend, or read a book, were drinking out of cups made from fossil fuels. Even when ordering a drink in a mug for here, I still got my drink in a throwaway plastic cup. I think there would be some simple fixes like being clear with your customers to see if they are staying for their beverage. And then making sure they get the reusable. Or perhaps, at least you can promote the availability of real mugs, or incentivize people to bring their own, on your socials. Because I see that you offer real plates for people eating a snack here, I can assume that you would have the capacity to offer and clean real mugs. But we cant continue to normalize such a rampant and reckless use of plastic every single day. Especially here in this waterfront city. Thank you for listening to my concerns, and thinking about how both the customer and the purveyor can work together toward a healthier Brooklyn. The plastic waste crisis will not solve itself, and we need businesses and customers on board to mitigate it.

For food I tried the Popcorn Chicken I finally got to try Double Chicken Please in SoHo, one of the top-tier bars in the world, and it truly lived up to the hype. I went on a weekday and lined up right after they opened. Since the bar is always fully booked online, walking in at opening time was the perfect way to get a seat without a reservation. The cocktail menu is creative and fun, divided into appetizers, entrees, and desserts, just like a food menu. We ordered three rounds so we could try each section. The cocktails are delicate, complex, and thoughtfully crafted. There were a few flavors we personally couldnt get into, but overall, the experience was definitely worth it. We also ordered some food to pair with the drinks, and it matched perfectly with the cocktails. If you want to experience a bar that ranks among the top ten in the world, I highly recommend giving Double Chicken Please a visit. Went to Double Chicken Please for dinner, and the experience was nothing short of interesting. The restaurant/bar opens at 5 PM, but even by 4:30 PM, there was already a snaking queue outside (refer to image). It took around 30 minutes before I reached the service staff at the front door. The space is divided into two sections Free Range and The Coop. Free Range serves as a casual waiting area and bar where you can enjoy creative cocktails before getting a table inside The Coop. At Free Range, the cocktails were truly unique inspired by cartoons and playful concepts. My friend and I tried Side Kart, Pocket of Sweets, and P.U.T.S. all of which were refreshingly inventive and fun. Once seated inside The Coop, we ordered the Salted Egg Yolk Chicken Sandwich. The chicken patty was large and nicely battered, but I wouldve preferred a more generous amount of salted egg yolk sauce. The drink menu here is arranged like a full-course meal starting with appetizers, then mains, and ending with dessert-inspired cocktails. We tried the Japanese Cold Noodles, and it was mind-blowing it tasted exactly like cold soba! Definitely the highlight of the night. We also tried the Key Lime Pie and Cold Pizza cocktails, but those were just okay in comparison. Overall, its a fun and creative concept, blending cocktails with culinary themes in a sleek setting. That said, the price point is on the higher side, and while it was worth experiencing once, I probably wouldnt rush back. Rating: 3.5/5 Came by to check out their drinks on a thursday night after work, you have to choose between the old menu or the new menu when you walk in. My group went with the new one in orange because the other old version in black would be a two hour wait, we had a party of five but could only get seats for 4 by the bar so we had to rotate seats and take turns standing on the side. The menu was really cool and there's a mini game you can play to help you randomly choose a drink, we all think it's rigged, because all of us kept getting the number 4, i ended up trying four because that was also what the bartender recommended as a fruity drink, but I didn't like it so I traded with my friend and he got like a coffee drink. Donut chicken sandwich looks cool but is skippable and has a spicy kick to it, i found out that it was coming from spicy pineapples which was interesting. Hot honey sandwich was pretty good. Our seats were still not ready for the other bar by the time we finished and every time we asked the waiter, it's always a 30 minutes or 45 minutes additional wait even though it's already past the two hours estimate we got since 6pm. The seating arrangement felt really messy. I think this is one of those places where you would try once and probably won't go back again for me. And definitely not worth the two hour wait. I also couldn't understand why they wouldn't just serve all the drinks at the place and would have to split it by the front and the back seating... that really didn't make sense.

reviews_text_combined: I had a great experience at Fish Cheeks. The only dish that didnt quite hit was the wok-fried morning glory, which was just okay compared to everything else. Overall, this place is a total gem and Id definitely come back! Overall this was not a good experience. The good: We ordered two appetizers and three entrees. We loved the mussel gorlae, which tasted like barbeque mussels with a delicious peanut based sauce. The mussels were cooked perfectly, which is not an easy feat. The entrees of the choo chee scallop was also cooked perfectly and the sauce was spicy and aromatic, it was our favorite dish of the night. We also got the grilled mackrel. It was light, refreshing, crunchy, sweet and salty. A perfect blend of flavors and our second favorite dish of the night. Servers were great and service was quick. The not so good: We also got the shrimp in 3 crab sauce. I personally found it to be way too acidic with no other elements to balance. I personally did not enjoy it but my partner did. The shrimps were a nice soft texture however. The crab curry was something we were looking forward to as we had had it at previous visits. It was okay. The crab was cooked nicely. The curry was just okay. It was lacking in flavor. It was also room temperature when it arrived at the table, I am not sure if its meant to be served like that. All this would have been okay and I would have given a 4/5 rating, but the worst part of this experience was the COLD! We were seated close to the door and we had our jackets on and freezing the entire time. We saw other diners who were also close to the door asking to move tables. We were never apologized to for the cold or offered anything. I think this was really poor hospitality on their part. The servers and hostesses were working hard and I know this is probably out of their control. I think management needs to remember why they are in this business. We rushed eating our dinner and didn't bother with dessert because we were so cold and just wanted to get out of there. Overall just not a great experience. Conclusion: I would recommend this restaurant if its a warm day or if you can specifically ask for a table far from the main entrance or upstairs but I won't be returning any time soon! One of the strongest Thai dinner programs in the city. I have been going to Fish Cheeks for several years so I have watched it change, expand, and evolve. I want to begin this review by noting that their menu seems to have been mellowed out flavor wise. While I am not certain if this is to appease the weak taste buds of the neighborhood or another reason, I unfortunately feel this has weakened the appeal of the restaurant for me. It is still delicious but I miss how much the spice here used to hit. On the most recent visit I ordered the steamed whole fish, the corn salad, the fried cabbage, and the 30 day aged beef. The meal begins with complimentary puffed rice cakes and a sweet dipping sauce. While not filling at all, it is a nice touch that elevates the experience and provides a warm welcome. The corn salad is delicious with a punchy acidic level and beautifully fresh vegetables. My only note is that the portion changes every single visit. Thankfully on this visit is was the largest we have ever had. It seems like a simple dish but it really hits the spot. While not ordered on this visit the zaab zaab wings are amazing. They taste like a more acidic taki seasoning on chicken wings. Again, the number of wings has changed each time we have ordered it so we decided against getting it this visit. A must order is one or the whole fish offerings (or all of them). On this visit we got the steamed fish. It is full of lime, cilantro and other fresh herbs and perfectly cooked. It is kept piping hot by a burner on the table. This fish is one of the best seafood preparations in the city. I could eat the entire thing myself even with how filling it is. The fried cabbage is a great side for the mains at this restaurant. Perfectly cooked while also being crispy fresh. We decided to get the 30 days aged beef this visit. It was fine but the flavored were mellow compared to everything else and the ground texture was a negative contrast with the lightness of the fish we ordered. I would get something else next time. The service was attentive and professional. With such a busy restaurant the hosts do a great job of managing the seating and turnover of tables. This restaurant is probably one of the best Ive been to in awhile. We ordered the Zaab Wings, the Fried Fish Garden Salad, and the Duck Curry. Everything arrived promptly but I never felt like they rushed us out. Food was extremely fresh and packed with flavor. The fried fish was my favorite, texture was perfectly crisp and it had so much flavor with just a hint of spice. The Zaab wings were also a standout, perfect texture with juicy meat inside, the spicy lime flavor had a really nice depth to it. I enjoyed the duck curry as well but didnt stand out as much as the other dishes and while I loved the taste, I felt the duck was a bit on the chewy side. Portions were great though for 2 people, everything is meant to be shared and we felt it was the perfect amount of food. Atmosphere was nice as well, I really loved their commitment to the fish decor, its nice when restaurants have a theme in my opinion, and the service was really good. My only complaint was it felt a little crowded, tables were pretty close together and it was a bit loud. Stopped in for lunch yesterday while in the area. The restaurant is super cute, vibes immaculate. I was expecting the food to be excellent and the flavors still blew me away. I probably could have downed a hundred oysters, but just ordered 2 as I was in a time crunch. Im not exactly sure that the toppings were but the texture and spices were new to me and were amazing. The half chicken was juicy and tasty, cant wait to try more of the menu as I am going to be in the neighborhood all week for work. Only downside was the barstools, looked great, but not very comfortable. Highly recommend this spot.

If it was out of 10 Id give this spot a 7 really to be fair. But yea try it out yourself and maybe your experience will be better who knows. Loved this brunch place. The airy, cloud-like texture of the pancakes sets them apart, and the cozy, modern atmosphere makes it a fun place to enjoy with friends. Service can be a bit slow during peak times, but its worth the wait. Flippers is a hidden gem. Stumbled upon it while looking for a place to eat as I was killing time from the passport office. Their menu is short but has various options. I ordered an omelet, that comes with an option of fries or salad, and a French Kissed mimosa. It was a hearty portion and was worth every single bite. The Brazilian caipiranha was very well made. deliciously sweet! it's definitely worth stopping in there for the happy hour. the bar was packed with the lunch time crew. ***This review is solely based on my first experience*** At the onset I feel the entrance needs to be moved, you walk in directly on people's dinning experience because there are tables right at the door. Next, our service was very poor. Our server was extremely unattentive. We waited for water then had to call him to us to order drinks and appetizers. reviews_text_combined: My boyfriend and I come here quite often. Service is always super fast and the staff is very friendly. Portion size is also decent, but its goona be hard to finish if you nibble on their focaccia too much;bc their focaccia is DELICIOUS. Springy texture with amazing olive oil taste, YUM. I wouldnt suggest coming with a large party of people because the seating area is small and cant really accommodate larger parties of 4 or more. Its definitely a must try! reviews_text_combined: Had a wonderful experience at Francie. Both the ambiance and the food were excellent the space feels elegant yet comfortable, perfect for a special night out. I would also highly recommend the clam pasta, which was flavorful and very satisfying. Overall, a great spot for a memorable dinner. Would happily come back again. The food here was incredible! I loved the bread and the sunflower butter! The lamb, bombolini, market salad, and the foi gras were so good! We also had the citron dessert which I loved! They have a great mocktail menu as well Francie is really all about the Duck, and the Pastas too! The starters vary between some misses like the bomboloni and carpaccio, but the Market salad was actually so horrible its not even pictured. But then theres the outstanding Roman Army Sourdough, this bread is perfect and comes with two nice spreads. The souffl cakes are tasty with a nice caviar butter spread. The Fussiloni was really good, a flavorful wintery sauce with some big bites and truffle. Then the Duck, its amazing, perfect roasted skin delivered with a huge portion in great sauce. Now, the fries are very great, but at any restaurant $20 for fries is a very high price and one that isnt even listed on the menu. Additionally, we had no real waiter most of the night, on a slow Tuesday night we had what seemed to be the manager for the first half of the night, then no waiter at all till dessert. The vibe inside is cool and overall the restaurant is fine, but why it has a Michelin Star doesnt really make sense compared to the objectively better restaurants in NYC that do or dont have one. We ordered a lot at Francie, but the clear highlights were the family duck ($152) and the steak ($225). Both arrived beautifully presented without feeling overdone. The steak had a perfect sear, tons of flavor, and was incredibly tender. The duck fully lived up to its reputation crisp, deeply golden skin, juicy meat, and a really clean, balanced plate. The vibe was polished but comfortable, not stuffy at all. Service was attentive and well paced, and nothing felt rushed. Definitely one of the better meals Ive had in Brooklyn and a place Id happily go back to. We booked dinner during restaurant week and we had a great experience. The food was very good and filling, even if the portions looked small! We had: - baby lettuces and fried artichokes for appetizers - bucatini al citron and rigatoni (a little spicy) - carrot cake and german chocolate cake We also had cocktails but I cant remember the name of it - it is on the restaurant week menu. Reminded me of the plane crash at Singers (bar in bedstuy)!

Dont stop run. Jesse: Hows everything? Hows the fish soup? Me: Well, its like an elevated Campbells soup. Mmm, but its good. First sip of the fish soup, feels like winter on the docks. Fin. also if you get Jesse, youre in luck. Hes an amazing person and also makes ordering the food an incredible experience. French fries to die for and mashed potatoes were just heavenly.

reviews_text_combined: As the pescatarian friend would love to see more seafood options (at all KBBQ places) but shrimp was tasty and filling for me. Gan Hoo in Flushing delivers consistently delicious food, with bold flavors and generous portions that make every meal satisfying. The dishes taste fresh and well-prepared, and you can tell the kitchen really knows what its doing. Whether youre ordering classic favorites or trying something new, the quality is always impressive. The one area where the experience falls short is the service. Its efficient, but very impersonal more transactional than welcoming. You get your food quickly and everything runs smoothly, but theres little warmth or engagement from the staff, which takes away from the overall dining experience. That said, the food alone makes it worth visiting. If youre looking for great Chinese cuisine and dont mind the lack of personal touch, Gan Hoo remains a reliable and tasty choice. The beef combo portion is a good size. The Wagyu fried rice is even better than the one I had in Japan. My kids love the unlimited French fries and chicken nuggets. Theres a reason we still had to wait 20 minutes at 3 p.m. (not even rush hour). This is one of the BBQ spots I find myself coming back to from time to time. The quality and flavor of the meat are well worth the price. Their watermelon juice and stair platter are must-orders, and there are plenty of other interesting menu items worth trying as well. Gan Hoo BBQ in Flushing brings the art of Asian grilling to another level, combining Korean, Japanese, and Chinese barbecue techniques in a single, unforgettable experience. The space glows with energy the moment you walk in the sizzle of meat on the grill, the laughter from nearby tables, and the aroma of perfectly marbled wagyu filling the air. Every cut that hits the fire feels like a performance, with flavors that build as the fat melts and the edges char just enough to give that perfect bite. The meats are high quality and beautifully presented, from the delicate wagyu slices to the crowd-favorite meat staircase, a showpiece that always draws a few cameras. Its one of those places where you go not only to eat, but to experience the rhythm of the meal the sharing, the grilling, the excitement of each plate arriving. The team moves quickly, making sure your grill stays hot and your table full, while the energy in the room keeps the night flowing effortlessly. Its lively, modern, and unapologetically fun, a perfect spot to come with friends, film a few sizzling shots for your feed, and lose yourself in the flavors. Gan Hoo BBQ captures what makes New Yorks dining scene so special that ability to transport you somewhere completely different while keeping its own bold personality. Its the kind of place that turns dinner into an experience, where every bite reminds you why food is meant to be shared.

I have travelled the world extensively over 40 years. I ate at all restaurants ranging from pop & moms to Michelin three stars. This is my honest review of the Genesis House in meatpacking district in New York, NY, USA. Genesis House is truly a hidden gem located on the second floor of the Genesis car dealership, which you access through the showroom, either by stairs or elevator. The restaurant offers both a tasting and an la carte menu; we went for the latter. We started with the beef tartare with egg yolk, beautifully seasoned with chili and tomato. It was spicy, creamy, and perfectly balanced an absolute success. The lobster rice followed, served with a salmon roll and a generous piece of lobster. Presented in a striking, almost artistic way, it revealed even more depth of flavor once mixed together. The short rib with serrano pesto and broccolini was likely the highlight of the evening tender, flavorful, and simply outstanding. For dessert, we tried the banana puff pastry with candied banana and banana milk ice cream a delicious explosion of flavor. We also enjoyed a soothing ginger and citrus infusion to finish the meal. Every dish was executed with precision and creativity worthy, in my opinion, of a Michelin star. The interior design is spectacular rich wood tones, refined architecture, and even a library seating area where guests remove their shoes and dine close to the floor in the traditional Korean way. While some dishes were fine, the burger and pork shoulder in particular were disappointing and just didnt hit the mark. The salad and mousse also felt a little ordinary. For a place with such a strong reputationand at this price pointI expected the dishes to shine more. Still, the service and vibe were wonderful, which made the experience enjoyable overall. If I had to rate it: 5 stars for service and ambiance, but about 3 stars for the food, so overall a solid 3 stars. Excellent dinner from start to finish. The seasonal menu was thoughtful and well executed, with great balance and high-quality ingredients. The lamb main was cooked perfectly and full of flavor, and the overall pacing of the meal felt just right. Desserts were refined and not overly sweet. Professional service and a relaxed, elegant atmosphere. Would definitely return. First off; special shout outs to Kamor and Morgan for exceptional service and for an exquisite experience. My daughter brother and I went out for my brothers birthday and had a phenomenal time. Thank you to the whole staff for their fluidity and expertise on every single dish that was presented. name: Grand Banks

reviews_text_combined: Overall, a highly recommended spot! The ambiance is lovely, the service attentive, and both the food and drinks are top-notch. The experience wasnt as dizzying as I had imagined, and the views are beautiful, with a glimpse of the Statue of Libertyperfect for a small gathering with friends. The drinks are consistently good, with no disappointments, and the lobster roll and oysters are definitely worth trying. Overall, a very enjoyable experience, reasonably priced, and I would gladly return. This place is fantastic! I have been here a few times for lunch with my coworkers and it is always a really good experience. The boat dos have days that it sways a lot, so if you are a person thats sensitive to motion sickness, then this is not the place for you. This is a seasonal restaurant, so they open in April and close towards end of October, depending on the weather. We ordered the lobster roll, scallops, spritz, and the chocolate dessert. All were really tasty! And dont forget the view! This place is absolutely awesome! The food and drinks made my day everything was so delicious that I couldnt get enough. The view was stunning, and the whole experience felt nostalgic, reminding me of the floating cottages in the Philippines. The atmosphere is calm and relaxing, perfect for anyone who enjoys dining by the sea. The staff were polite, easy to talk to, and made me feel really comfortable throughout my visit. However, I wouldnt recommend it for people who are sensitive to motion sickness, since the restaurant is on a boat and can sway a bit. If you love great food, refreshing drinks, and a beautiful waterfront view, I highly recommend making a reservation here. You wont regret it! glad we tried this place because they are super dog friendly, everyone was so welcoming. its super busy so you definitely need to reserve. the view is beautiful and the boat is nice, though it does rock and make you queasy. the drinks were delicious but took 45 mins to arrive, oysters came first and quickly. food took forever to order and even longer to arrive and it was mid tier at best. good lobster roll and good scallops but so overpriced. service was nonexistent. server took forever though she was really nice. had to flag someone else down to try and get our server and they said no words just walked away. price exorbitant given the quality you receive. The oysters were incredibly fresh, and every dish we tried had that perfect balance of flavor and elegance simple, but done so right. The ambiance is laid-back yet refined, and the staff was welcoming and attentive, I'd only pay attention when reserving bc it can get really hot and uncomfortable to seat in the sun at noon so make sure you get a table with shadow or at the bar if you want to enjoy lunch not sweaty!! name: Grimaldi's Pizzeria
description: Casual, Brooklyn-based pizzeria chain serving brick-oven pies & calzones, plus wine & beer.
formatted_address: 1 Front St, Brooklyn, NY 11201, USA
matched_google_address: 1 Front St, Brooklyn, NY 11201, USA
reviews_text_combined: Grimaldi's under the Brooklyn Bridge is where the New York pizza argument starts and ends for me. Coal-fired oven thin crust that has that perfect char and chew fresh mozzarella that stretches and pulls exactly right and a tomato sauce that is bright and balanced without overpowering the cheese. This is not fancy pizza. This is not artisan pizza with truffle oil and microgreens. This is New York pizza done the way it was meant to be done and it is perfect. The line out the door is legendary and yes you will wait but the wait is part of the experience. Standing on the cobblestones of DUMBO looking up at the Brooklyn Bridge while you wait for one of the best pies in the city that is a New York moment. Cash only no slices whole pies only. You commit to the experience and the experience rewards you. The dining room is simple and packed and the energy is fantastic families tourists locals who have been coming here for decades all sitting elbow to elbow enjoying the same thing. I have eaten pizza in Naples Rome Chicago and everywhere in between. Grimaldi's holds its own against all of them. The simplicity is the genius. Great dough great sauce great cheese great oven. Do four things perfectly and you do not need a fifth. That is Grimaldi's and that is New York. We needed to recharge after a three hours walking tour in Brooklyn. As we entered, the inviting warmth of the restaurant. You can't beat the location or the flavor at Grimaldi's Pizzeria under the Brooklyn Bridge. We ordered the 18" split, with additional cost, featuring the signature BROOKLYN BRIDGE (ricotta, sausage, peppers--perfection!) and the beautifully simple MARGHERITA. The split was recommended by the server as we couldn't decide one toppings. The staff was wonderful! It was outstanding! Truly one of the best food experiences we had visiting New York! Came here after waiting outside to enter another well known pizza joint nearby. At first I was a little concerned that Grimaldis would be a tourist trap at the expense of the food quality but I neednt have worried. My 16 inch margarita pizza with mushrooms (kept it simple) came out steaming, with a crispy base and fresh tomato source. Cant say like other reviewers whether it was the best pizza Ive ever tasted, but it really was delicious. Servers were courteous and atmosphere nice. Grimaldis serves traditional coal-fired brick oven pizza. This is the most famous Grimaldis location, right next door to its original location. I tried the Brooklyn Bridge, which comes with roasted red peppers, creamy ricotta, Italian sausage, mozzarella, and a tomato sauce base, and The Don, topped with Italian sausage, meatballs, pepperoni, mozzarella, and tomato sauce. The ricotta on the Brooklyn Bridge wasnt really my thing, but The Don was delicious. I also had their red sangria, which was very refreshing, and finished off with the Oreo cheesecake for dessert. Highly recommend! They offer three pizza sizes: 12, 16, and 18. We ordered two 18-inch pizzas to share between four people. Even though my friends were pretty hungry, we still ended up with leftovers. In hindsight, two 16-inch pizzas wouldve been the perfect amount. We went on a Saturday around 3 p.m. and waited about 30 minutes outside before being seated, so definitely expect a line if you visit on the weekend. Great food, long wait. We got there early (about 11:15am on a Monday) and waited 10 minutes to be seated. However, we waited over an hour for our single pizza. Once we got it, the Chef special "The Don" was delicious!!! We also tried the cannoli--with the powdered sugar, it was very sweet! Cost was incredibly reasonable...one of our cheapest restaurants, including fast food. As we left around 12:45pm, the line extended about a half a block out the door.

reviews_text_combined: Fun spot for some authentic food. The chicken was fresh, the margarita was delicious, and the grilled corn was amazing. There's a reason it's "world-famous." reviews_text_combined: Tried this place over the Christmas holiday weekend. The food was very good. Alot of choices to choose from. Meats were fresh and tasty. The bone marrow broth was yummy. Plus, the dessert on the premium menu is bioengineered ice cream. :) If you care about food quality and your health, Id recommend going somewhere else. This is our favorite spot ever! I think we go once a month. Everything is so fresh from the meat to the sauces and to the incredible vegetables. They have such a variety of choices and everything in the buffet is prepped well and high quality. I highly recommend doing their premium option so you can order wagyu. Youre the best happy lamb! Ive been coming to Happy Lamb for 5 years and always loved it. Even though its all-you-can-eat and you basically do most of the work yourself, my partner and I always tip 20% no matter what. We usually get the premium option too, even though its honestly kind of overpriced, because we genuinely enjoyed coming here. Last week, though, I had a really upsetting experience. I went to grab a bowl to serve myself some rice, which is something weve done every single time weve come over the past 5 years. A woman who turned out to be a server started yelling at me for touching the bowls. Not politely correcting me - actually yelling. The first time, I let it go. The second time, she yelled again. Later, when I was calmly explaining the situation to my partner at our table, she came over and started yelling again. Thats when my partner finally told her to let it go because she was making it worse. It was completely unnecessary and extremely unprofessional. After I started bawling my eyes out, she later came over to say sorry - but at that point, it honestly felt too late. If I hadnt broken down, would she have just continued yelling? It didnt feel like a genuine apology; it felt like damage control. What makes this even more disappointing is that I had received some bad news earlier that morning. My partner brought me there because hotpot has always been a comfort place for us. Instead of leaving feeling better, I left crying and completely overwhelmed. After 5 years of loyalty, this experience was enough for us to decide were never going back. Its really sad that such rude and aggressive behavior cost them long-time customers. reviews_text_combined: Harlem Shake was absolutely delicious! I went there on a Saturday night and was pumped that it wasnt too over crowded and that I didnt have to wait forever to get my food. We ordered at the counter and then grabbed a booth inside - and got to see a lot of great signed photos of celebrities and entertainers who had visited this location along the walls. My friend got the yam fries that were even better than the regular fries! Highly recommending going to this place when youre in the area! If I lived nearby Id be getting takeout or delivery from here all the time! I visited Harlem Shake in late September and early October 2025. Ive always walked by this restaurant while in the area and always wanted to try it. I finally decided to give it a try. When we entered the restaurant, we were greeted by staff. When I visited again in October, I ordered the Classic Fried Chicken Sandwich and it had absolutely no flavor. I only tasted black pepper. It was too bland for such a chucky piece of chicken. I was very disappointed. I don't think I'd order this again. I ordered another hot dog but forgot to say I wanted it well done. It was okay this time they put the right amount of ketchup but again way to much sauerkraut. I decided to try their Shirley Temple Soda. Im warning you now do not order this. It was a little more on the expensive side, but not overly so. I would definitely come back to try other things from the menu. Food was ok. Fairly priced. A piece of history and delicious food in one place.

Its the kind of place longtime Chinatown regulars return to week after week, and where newcomers quickly learn that authenticity doesnt need explanationit simply shows up on the table, steaming, familiar, and deeply satisfying. reviews_text_combined: The story of Jacob Soul Food Restaurant is a heartwarming tale of community, family dedication, and an immigrant's dream realized in the heart of Harlem. The restaurant, located at 373 Malcolm X Blvd (Lenox Avenue), was founded by Rachid Niang, an entrepreneur from Senegal who was educated in France before making Harlem his home. He named the restaurant after his late father, Jacob, and it quickly became a neighborhood favorite after opening in 2009. The Great Story: Giving Back on Thanksgiving The most enduring story of Jacob's is its commitment to giving back to the community, which started just months after its first location opened. Despite being a brand new business, Niang felt compelled to show gratitude to the community that was supporting his dream. On their first Thanksgiving, he opened the doors of his restaurant and invited anyone in Harlemregular customers and those who couldn't afford a hearty holiday mealto dine for free. This tradition has continued for over a decade. Every Thanksgiving Day, Rachid Niang and his wife, Kim Lum Chan Niang, mobilize dozens of volunteers to serve hundreds of free, traditional holiday and soul food meals. Guests can choose from classic Southern dishes like roast turkey, candied yams, and macaroni and cheese, along with Jacob's everyday Caribbean and soul food favorites. The restaurant, known for its buffet-style selection of Southern, Caribbean, and Continental fare, is not just a place to eat; it's a true community hub built on family heritage and a deep connection to Harlem. Note on Location: The restaurant you are referring to is located at 373 Lenox Avenue (which is also known as Malcolm X Boulevard) in Harlem, New York, NY 10027.
