The vibe is classy but not I need a tie, the service is confident without hovering, and the smell alone should be illegal without a warning label. The ribs were perfection!!! Lets just say my knife did less work than I did chewing gum as a kid. Perfectly cooked, seasoned like the chef knows your ancestors, and so tender it basically apologized before melting in my mouth. Sides are not afterthoughts here theyre supporting actors stealing the scene. Everything arrived hot, fresh, and clearly prepared by people who care deeply about flavor and your happiness. I ordered the wedge saland and the blue cheese dressing was the best in the western hemisphere! Also the macaroni and cheese was so good we ordered another bowl for desert!! The woman that sat us and served us deserves a standing ovation. Friendly, knowledgeable, and somehow always there exactly when you need here like steakhouse ninja. Bottom line: Come hungry. Leave happy. Question why you dont eat here more often. If youre celebrating something, this is the place. If youre not celebrating something, you will be by the time dessert shows up. I didnt just have dinner I had an experience. 10/10. Would loosen my belt again. One of the best steaks I have ever had. This Bone-in Ribeye was super buttery and soft! I could cut into it with a fork. Also the sides were amazing, perfect pair with the steak. Joining the Army in a couple days so this was an amazing "last" dinner. When visiting NYC from Florida, we took the Staten Island Ferry for the first time to go to a show in the St. George Theatre. We met up here for dinner and were all pleasantly surprised at how awesome dinner. Walk into a not fancy place, but warm and cozy like home, which makes sense because it is a great family run restaurant that smells so delicious inside. The service was attentive and we all had wonderful meals. The family we met here for dinner, said they will be back here again!

name: Rule of Thirds
description: Creative Japanese comfort fare by a lauded restaurant group in a cozy setting with tall ceilings.
formatted_address: 171 Banker St, Brooklyn, NY 11222, USA
matched_google_address: 171 Banker St, Brooklyn, NY 11222, USA
reviews_text_combined: We had the Skate Wing, Oshizushi, House Silken Tofu, Clams Sakamushi, and Wagyu Sukiyaki. Every dish was earthy, nourishing, and beautifully balanced, made with very fresh ingredients and delicate flavors. The presentation of each dish really added to the overall experience. We also had sencha green tea served in a teapot, and the taste wow. It was so good that I even asked the waiter what kind of tea it was. Now I actually have the same one at home. The staff was excellent attentive, welcoming, and very knowledgeable. At one point we accidentally spilled some tea or sauce on the table, and they immediately cleaned it up with a smile, which we really appreciated. Throughout the evening we felt very well informed, welcomed, and genuinely taken care of. The music created a great atmosphere, and the whole experience felt close to perfection. Well definitely be coming back to try more dishes especially the duck, which looked incredible. Beautiful space with beautiful food that honestly just tasted fine. Like everything was good but nothing really blew me away, until the dessert that was stellar. Generally shared everything to get a taste of a bunch of things. To start with, the Karaage was really crisp and had a nice sauce. The Oshizushi tasted fresh. House Silken Tofu was very interesting, super duper soft almost like a pudding. Also got the Almond Miso Soup which was divine. Unfortunately did not taste as much almond butter as Id hoped but the broth was incredibly flavorful and super creamy. Tried a few of the Binchotan and sides too. Chicken Meatball had a nice marinade. Macaroni Salad was a large portion. Yaki Onigiri was one of my favorite items despite literally just being rice, but the char and the chicken fat wash made it so delish. Then came dessert which was def the best dishes. The Rice Cooker Cheesecake was so light and airy and the pumpkin, caramel, and persimmons were such a good touch. Then the Yuzu Soft Serve was one of the best soft serves Ive ever had. Sweet and super tart too, perfect level of creamy too I just couldnt stop eating it. Also the soft serve with the cheesecake in the same bite was a legendary combo. Everything was GREAT. My boyfriend and I had the karaage, gyu don, and the souffle pancake with a yuzu mini soft serve. The Karaage was crispy and fresh, I think this may be the best Karaage Ive had in a long time. The sauces had deep flavors that truly added to the experience The gyudon was also great! I usually dont get to enjoy gyudons because I find the meat too fatty and at times it feels like theres more onion than beef. I did not find either of these issues with our gyudon. The pancake was better than I expected. As a texture person I thought it would be too mushy for my taste, instead it was fluffier than I ever imagined. The honey butter added enough sweetness so I only used the maple syrup occasionally. The yuzu soft serve was a 10/10 in my opinion but my boyfriend found it too citrusy. The atmosphere was wonderful, the staff was amazing and even though the place was busy our water cups were never empty. Shout out to Zack for making our experience so awesome. Was really excited to try this place, have been wanting to go here for a year. My boyfriend and I got reservations for Valentines Day. We had recently come back from a trip to Japan, I am Japanese for context. The food was underwhelming. Tasted like vague Japanese flavor with French or American influence. Not what I thought the restaurant would be. Did not feel catered or for Asian people, even though it is Asian cuisine. I would not recommend the black bass, macaroni salad, skewers, or almond miso. My favorite was the Wagyu beef tartare with the house bread. Very fun and fresh flavors. The service was really lovely and the ambiance was great! Since we were celebrating Vday/our anniversary, they brought us some non alcoholic wine to taste. Fun! We will be returning to try the brunch to see if it can redeem this dinner. Will also have lower expectations because its clearly not Japanese food, but Japanese inspired for the Brooklyn American palate. The menu is so thoughtful including the drinks. We loved trying skate wing for the first time. the Crab Mazemen was our fav dish of the night. The Zabuton Steak was incredible as well! The mushroom special left a little to be desired but it was still solid. The restaurant is beautiful, the staff is so lovely (shoutout to Julio) and we will be coming back to try brunch very soon!

name: Russ & Daughters
description: Family-owned shop offering high-end smoked fish, caviar & New York-style specialty foods since 1914.
formatted_address: 179 E Houston St, New York, NY 10002, USA
matched_google_address: 179 E Houston St, New York, NY 10002, USA
reviews_text_combined: I finally got a chance to stop by Russ & Daughters, and now I totally understand why its such an iconic New York institution. Stepping inside, the whole place has this classic, timeless, old-school deli vibe, like walking back into a genuine piece of history. All the beautiful fish and items displayed behind the counter are stunning. In true New York fashion, they move fast, so definitely come prepared to order! The food quality here is absolutely next level. Everything they serve is incredibly fresh and flavorful. I went with a classic, getting the salmon sandwich on an everything bagel, and the fish was just exceptionalso fresh, packed with flavor, and sliced generously. The classic bagels and spreads are a perfect complement, with the cream cheese being so good it honestly steals the show! You truly get a lot of food for what you order, making the whole experience feel completely worthwhile and a great value. I did notice that because they are so popular, the service can feel slightly slow when they are slammed, but honestly, the quality of the smoked fish and the authentic atmosphere more than make up for any wait time. Russ & Daughters is a must-visit for anyone who appreciates top-tier smoked fish and an authentic deli experience. Its sought out by locals for a reason, and I can't wait to go back. The lox here was good, but the bagel was not When people bring up the best bagel & lox sandwich in NYC, this place comes up occasionally. However, having had it, when you only do half that equation well, I don't think you can be in consideration On the positive side, the smoked salmon was pretty appetizing. It tasted very fresh and had a great salt level. It did feel a bit thin in the context of the bagel, though, like there wasn't that much of it. The cream cheese was fine, but nothing out-of-this-world. The bagel was where I had a big problem. It was so chewy, so not-fresh, that it was not easy to eat. I could tell after my first bite that this was nothing like the other fresh bagels I've had in the city. It just tasted like it was baked a long time ago, and transported to the restaurant. I had to give up on eating this as a sandwich and eventually had to eat it like an open-faced bagel on each half, because of how I just couldn't deal with having that much bad bagel in a single bite. It was just not good. The other thing I've heard people say is the right thing is just to buy the smoked salmon from here, buy a bagel from somewhere else, and make your own DIY bagel & lox. After having this, I would recommend that as well. It cost $18 - which is not terrible, and the portion size is pretty good, but I wouldn't call this a value either Overall, it's some good lox mixed with a pretty bad bagel. 5/10 Just heaven on earth! I recently visited Russ & Daughters and had the most unbelievable Pastrami Russ. The pastrami-cured salmon on an everything bagel, paired with mustard and sauerkraut, was an absolute revelation, bold, perfectly balanced, and unforgettable. I even brought some of their chocolate-dipped coconut macaroons back home to Texas and cant wait to try them. Truly a must-visit and highly recommended! Take a number when you walk in the door. The bagels here are top notch and there are plenty of high end options for toppings, take home items and sides. The pickles are some of the best I've ever had and the lox cream cheese is unbelievable. We are our bagels across the street in the park, it's take out only. I researched where a NY visitor could find a classic Jewish deli and Russ & Daughters did not disappoint. This deli has been around since the 1920s with fun memorabilia to show the history. On a Friday at 11am, we had no wait because we sat at the bar, but there was not much of a line for a seat. The menu had many yummy dishes almost all included salmon and other traditional cured fish. If you love fish in your food, you will love Russ & Daughters as they do not skimp on the portions. This was a great NYC breakfast icon that is worth the trip.

name: Sadelle's New York

reviews_text_combined: Sadelles never disappoints. We ordered the fish tower with white fish, lox, and smoked fisheverything was incredibly fresh and beautifully presented. We had their everything bagels and it was crispy and fresh. We definitely recommend. Bloody mary was good but not a must. Delicious! So happy we finally made it to Sadelles- its been sitting at the top of my must-try list forever, and it completely lived up to my expectations. From the moment we walked in, the space had that charming, old-school caf feel: cozy, bustling, and full of that classic New York energy without feeling chaotic. We started with the classic lox, and wow- absolutely incredible. I loved how thinly it was sliced; you could taste the silky texture and fresh flavor in every bite. Paired with their iconic bagels (perfectly crisp on the outside, soft and chewy inside), its exactly what you want a New York bagel experience to be. The cream cheese was amazing too! The French toast deserves its own paragraph because it was that good- easily the best Ive ever had. Thick, golden, and somehow rich without being heavy. Every bite tasted like pure breakfast magic. The breakfast potatoes were another pleasant surprise: crispy edges, soft centers, and seasoned just right. Service was great from start to finish. Our server was friendly, helpful with recommendations, and checked in just enough without hovering. It made the whole meal feel relaxed and enjoyable. Overall, Sadelles absolutely delivered. I cant wait to go back and try even more of the menu. Just a heads-up: definitely make a reservation- this place gets busy, and now I fully understand why. Loved everything about it. Sadelles is my go-to brunch spot. The bagels are fresh and perfect every single time which matters, especially when youre ordering breakfast and salmon can lowkey be risky. Their quality is actually insane, and everyone I go with always says the same thing. The smoked salmon that comes with the house tower is my always order. So good. The mimosas are amazing too, and their other drinks never disappoint. Ill be honest though the tomato soup was not my vibe at all, and the coffee didnt do it for me either. But the bagels alone make it worth going. The French toast is good but kinda overhyped in my opinion. Its good, just not wow Im losing my mind good. The pancakes though?? Soft, fluffy, sooo good. Overall, Sadelles is still a great brunch spot. Always good vibes when I walk in.

name: Saiguette
description: Popular for banh mi & pho, this snug spot also offers Vietnamese entrees for eat-in or takeout.
formatted_address: 935 Columbus Ave, New York, NY 10025, USA
matched_google_address: 935 Columbus Ave, New York, NY 10025, USA
reviews_text_combined: Came in for the Pork Belly Banh Mi and was not disappointed by any means. Bread was crispy on the outside and soft on the inside as it sopped up the juices from the pickled veggies and the sauce. The sauce is mild on the spice with a little tang which complimented both the pickled veggies and pork belly incredibly. Last but certainly not least, the star of the show, the pork belly was sooo tender it basically just melts in your mouth. 10/10 definitely recommend! Saiguette is one of those hidden gems in NYC that consistently delivers when it comes to Vietnamese comfort food, especially for takeout. Ive tried a good amount of Vietnamese spots in the city, and while Saiguette doesnt offer a dine in experience (its strictly takeout), they make up for it with flavor, portion size, and speed. The banh mi here is definitely the standout. Unlike some places that skimp on the fillings, Saiguette really loads it up. The bread is fresh with the perfect balance of crunch on the outside and softness inside, and the fillings are generous you dont feel like youre paying just for bread. I also appreciated that the balance of meat, pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs is done right, giving it that satisfying bite every time. Portion wise, its more than fair for the price, especially considering NYC standards where you often pay more for less. Pho and other noodle dishes also come packed with flavor. The broth is rich, and the portions again dont leave you hungry. For a takeout-only spot, the packaging is practical and sturdy it travels well, and you can enjoy it at home without everything spilling or getting soggy. The one downside for me was more about the ownership and authenticity. When I asked if the restaurant was Vietnamese owned, one of the workers told me yes. Later on, I found out that the owner is not Vietnamese, which was a bit disappointing, especially after I tried speaking Vietnamese to him and realized it wasnt the case. It doesnt take away from the taste of the food, but as someone who values authenticity and cultural roots, it felt misleading. Overall, though, Saiguette is worth trying if youre in NYC and craving Vietnamese food. The quality, portion size, and fair pricing make it a solid choice, especially for banh mi lovers. While I wish the ownership had been upfront about the background, I cant deny that the food itself is good and consistent. Saiguette has a really excellent Banh Mi. The lemongrass pork shoulder is my absolute favorite, the spicy creamy sauce is delicious and the pork is juicy. The veggies inside are generously portioned and the bread is crispy and soft. I probably eat it once a week and its genuinely almost a perfect sandwich. Incredible pork shoulder lemongrass Banh Mi! Best I have ever had. The fresh bread is delicious, perfect flavour balance. Can't recommend enough. i went during lunch time so it was 10% off the Vietnamese iced coffee was strong and sweet. The pork sandwich was delish and so filling. The place doesn't have any indoor seating. however, its 2 blocks from central park

name: SARAVANAA BHAVAN
description: Long-running international chain serving up South Indian vegetarian dishes, including dosas.
formatted_address: 81 Lexington Ave, New York, NY 10016, USA
matched_google_address: 81 Lexington Ave, New York, NY 10016, USA
reviews_text_combined: Great South Indian comfort food. The pav bhaji was rich and buttery with nicely toasted bread. The Mysore masala dosa came perfectly crispy with a flavorful kick from the chutney, and even the plain dosa was fresh, light, and satisfying. The mirchi bhaji was crunchy, not greasy, and had just the right level of heat. Solid quality, tasty chutneys and sambar Id happily come back. Saravanaa Bhavan is not just a restaurantits a feeling thats hard to put into words. The moment you walk in, theres a sense of familiarity and comfort, especially if youve grown up with South Indian food. It instantly takes you back homeearly morning breakfasts, hot dosas, the aroma of ghee, sambar simmering, and simple, soulful meals made with love. The food tastes exactly the way it shouldauthentic, consistent, and comforting. Every bite feels nostalgic, reminding you why Saravanaa Bhavan has earned its name across the world. Theres no unnecessary fusion or overdoingjust honest South Indian flavors served the way theyve always been. For many of us living away from home, this place isnt just about eatingits about connection, memories, and comfort. A meal here feels like a warm hug from home. Truly deserving of 5 stars, every single time. Excellent as always! The food was delicious and the service was great. We had ordered ghee roast masala dosa, dahi vada, milagaipodi masala dosa (spicy), and obviously filter coffee. The dosas were crispy and fresh,and the coffee was spot on. The menu is pretty descriptive and is properly marked about spice levels, vegan and gluten free options. Its perfect for first time visitors as well. Highly recommended! Stopped by for lunch and the masala dosa really hit the spot crispy outside, perfectly spiced potato filling. Also tried the rose milk, which was refreshingly sweet and a nice complement to the meal. Service was quick and the place has a casual, no-frills vibe that works well for South Indian comfort food. Definitely worth a visit if you're craving authentic vegetarian fare. Saravanaa Bhavan is one of the most well-known Chennai-based South Indian vegetarian restaurant chains, with locations all over the world, including New York and New Jersey. I visited this New York location, and as always, it was buzzing with a mixed crowd not just Indians, but many non-Indians who clearly love the food here too. They primarily serve classic Tamil-style South Indian dishes like dosa, idli, uttapam, rasam, sambar, and thalis. We went on a cold day around lunchtime and luckily got a table right away. I started with a bowl of rasam, and it was perfect hot, tangy, comforting, and exactly what I needed as I wasnt feeling my best. It came with papadum and felt truly rejuvenating. I also had a cup of filter coffee, which was rich, creamy, and strong just how South Indian coffee should be. For the main meal, we ordered masala dosa with potato filling and gunpowder kind of podi sprinkled on top, along with an onion dosa. Both were served with multiple chutneys and sambar, everything piping hot, fresh, flavorful, and made with quality ingredients. It was hearty, filling, and absolutely satisfying even if its definitely carb-loaded! Overall, it was a really wonderful experience. If you love authentic South Indian vegetarian food, this place is definitely worth a visit.

name: Scarr's Pizza
description: Pizzeria with retro looks serving pies, slices & subs made with modern ingredients.
formatted_address: 35 Orchard St, New York, NY 10002, USA
matched_google_address: 35 Orchard St, New York, NY 10002, USA
reviews_text_combined: Get here early! We arrived a few minutes before they opened and there was already a line. The people who work here are superhuman. They move so quickly and efficiently even though there isnt much room. Its dazzling to watch them make pies, throw things in the oven, and cut slices. We got the vodka pesto, original, and Sicilian hotboi slices. The original was simple and just right. Good sauce-to-cheese ratio, thickness, and taste. The other slices were also amazing. No regrets, no notes. One of the best slices Ive had in the city. Saw some reviews say that you can only sit if you order a full pie. We were able to sit at the bar with just slices, so YMMV. Pizzas were delicious and what I expected from NY pizza with the nice crust. The plain slice was perfect but the hot slice was a bit greasy/soggy that the dough tore apart a bit when I lifted it. Service was quick since I went for lunch. However, they assumed I wanted a medium size beer when I wanted the small glass. Also, this restaurant has changed since the last time I went. It's been a while. Now, you either order takeout or dine in with waiter service. Back then, you can just order at the counter and eat it inside without waiter service. It is awkward that to dine in a pizza joint that comes with waiter service, though it is also unfeasible to do takeout during the winter, yet they serve the pizza slices on paper plates. Some restaurants should not be waiter service. Feels like they just want to make a few extra dollars making it waiter service so that more people will give tip money. It should be the old format: order standing up, get your slices of pizza, sit at a table without waiter service, finish in 5 minutes and leave. It's so awkward to have this kind of environment at a pizza joint. This pizza was pretty excellent, and the classic embodiment of what you think of as a 'New York slice' I got the beef pepperoni as my NY-style slice, and the mushroom as the Sicilian-style slice here They were both very good, but I'll start with the NY slice first What was the most noticeable here was how good the crust was. It was very cracker-like, maybe the most cooked undercarriage I've seen on pizza, without being burnt. It perfectly exemplifies the term "well-charred". I thought I preferred softer crusts to harder ones, but after having had this slice, it's definitely the opposite for me - once I realized how good a crust like this could be. Aside from the crust, the rest of this slice was also fantastic. The blend of cheese and sauce on the top tastes great, especially those bits where you see the browned cheese on top. The pepperoni was also super consistent; basically every piece was able to curl up into a cup on top The Sicilian style pizza was also nice. The dough was way more airy and softer here, but it worked as a different type of pizza. The part that I liked slightly less was that it was a bit sloppy as not all the toppings stayed on throughout, and I preferred the level of cook in the other slice, but the softer taste was a nice change of pace. For the price, each slice cost me around $5, which is not *super* cheap, but when you consider that these are a) meat/special slices and b) quite large, I will take that deal Overall, if people ask me where to find a great New York-style slice, this will be the first place I point to. 9/10 We were in town Super Bowl Sunday, there was a decent crowd in there. But I loved that we were still able to get a table. We shared a slice of the hotboi pizza because we were already going around trying all kinds of food that day. But that pizza was amazing and it was actually spicy which I loved. They also have cocktails and I got the canned cocktail which was pretty good too. The music and the vibes were fun, I'd definitely go back when I'm in NYC. Scarrs Pizza in the Lower East Side definitely lives up to the hype. The pizza has a really nice char on the crust not too soggy, not too firm just that perfect NYC balance. You can really taste the quality of the ingredients in every bite. There can be a bit of a wait, but the line moves quickly and is worth it once you get your slice. The only small downside is that they dont have fountain soda, which is kind of a New York pizzeria staple. The seating area looks nice, but I chose not to sit because there was quite a bit of smoke in the dining room coming from the ovens. Overall, great pizza and worth the stop if youre in the area just maybe grab your slice to go.

name: Semma

reviews_text_combined: Getting a reservation at Semma in NYC is one of the hardest in the city, but after finally dining there, its easy to see why. Every dish felt thoughtful, bold, and deeply rooted in authentic South Indian flavors while still presented in a refined, elevated way. The lobster dish was one of the biggest highlights of the meal. The lobster was perfectly cooked and coated in a rich, creamy spiced sauce that had incredible depth and balance. It was indulgent, flavorful, and truly unforgettable. The dosa was beautifully crisp and golden, with that perfect fermented tang. Paired with the chutneys and accompaniments, it was simple yet executed flawlessly. The lamb curry completely stole the show. Served bone-in with a deeply aromatic gravy, the lamb was incredibly tender and packed with bold spices. Eating it with the flaky parotta made every bite comforting and rich easily one of the best dishes of the night. Beyond the food, what made the experience even more special was meeting the chef at the end of the meal. Despite running one of the most sought-after restaurants in NYC, he was incredibly down-to-earth, warm, and humble, taking the time to chat with guests which made the experience feel even more memorable. Semma has been on my list forever, so I was thrilled when my partner managed to score a coveted reservation for my birthday dinner last night. We both love Indian food, and this was a total hit from start to finish. Service was lovely, and they gave us a great table for two tucked into a nice corner of the restaurant. Every dish was beautifully composed and had just the right amount of spice. We started with the mung beans, which were bright, spicy, and a perfect way to kick things off. That was followed by their signature dosa, which was incredible. Ive had my share of dosas, and this one really stood outpaper thin, crispy, and full of flavor. Next came the rabbit leg, lobster curry, and lamb chops. Each dish was unique, but the lamb was probably my favorite of the nightso tender and served in a wonderfully savory gravy. We also ordered a parotta, which was buttery and flaky and the perfect way to mop up all the sauces. We finished by sharing the coconut dessert, which was light, refreshing, and a perfect ending after so much food. Such a great evening. Ill definitely be back. Amazing food! Prices are very fair for a 1 Michelin star restaurant. Chef came out and greeted every table individually. Highly recommend the gunpowder dosa! Only downside is the difficulty in getting reservations/waiting time for bar seating. Highly recommend if you can snag a table! Reservations required. Michelin star rated, high expectations. Did not disappoint, attentive wait staff, we tried quite a few dishes. Favorites were the dosa and the lamb chops. They were amazing. Presentation of food was delicious. Lamb was so tender and flavorful. We did pre order the crab, it get a little messy, but was delicious. Spice level was on the lower end for Indian food in my opinion, but was still delicious. From the appetizers to the desserts, recommend all of it. Keeping it real, this place was just average, maybe 2/5 stars. Given its Michelin-starred status, I expected outstanding cuisine, but it failed to impress. If you're a Desi craving Indian food, this isn't the place. The innovative take on South Indian food was interesting, but lacked depth and story. Its a decent place to try, but I wouldnt go out of my way to make a reservation to have to eat here. Service was also on par with the theme of the place, which is meant to resemble that of a street side restaurant loud and bustling.

Food quality is generally high, but some have found the chicken bites greasy and the milkshake average. Overall, Shake Shack Dumbo is a great option for a quick, delicious meal with a view. Just be prepared for potential wait times and variable food quality. One of the best locations in the city the view is to die for. The service was great even though they where short staffed that night we received timely table service the night before the storm. Delicious burgers and fries as expected of Shake Shack. The plus point for this particular eatery was it spacious and not crowded even during lunch time on the weekday we were there. And service was quick! Burger tastes as savory and flavorful as usual. Fried were clearly seasoned and tasted phenomenal, with and without sauce. Considering how many people were in there and that they cook the burgers fresh, I would say I got my food in a good wait time. I'm definitely coming back! 5/5 Classic Comfort with a View! We had an amazing experience at the Shake Shack Dumbo location honestly, everything about it hit the spot and made our day in Brooklyn even better. First off, you cant beat the location. Nestled right in the heart of Dumbo, just steps from the waterfront and the iconic views of the Manhattan Bridge its the perfect place to stop for a bite while exploring the area. The atmosphere here is casual but lively, and you really feel like youre part of the NYC energy tourists and locals all mingling, enjoying good food with great vibes. We ordered 2 single ShackBurgers and 2 orders of spicy cheese fries, and wow. Shake Shack always delivers on consistency, but this location felt extra fresh and flavorful. The burgers were perfectly cooked juicy, hot, and full of that classic Shack sauce flavor we love. The buns were super soft and slightly toasted, just how they should be. reviews_text_combined: 17 bucks each drink pay by cash: Afro Trip and Mango Mojito. They are not good. We should have gone with beer. Afro Trip is simply a mixed of beer and rum blended with too much ice and Mango Mojito is too bitter since they just squeeze all the lime hard with the outer skin. The music is neutral, nothing special. Sometimes the band do not sound harmonized... It was Sunday night so maybe the line-ups are not the best. The saving only saving point is the decoration and the vibe. Great vibes, quaint bar, surprisingly good food! Went for a jazz night and the bands were great! Got some wine and cocktails and food all reasonably priced. My friend got the muscles and I got the salmon dish and it was large amount of food. Everything tasted fantastic as well. Looking forward to hitting this legendary spot again! Literally such an amazing spot! I had the best time ever. The artists lineup was so good and the atmosphere felt like a movie. The people that go there are super friendly and lovely aswell. Great vibes all around. I didnt try the food so Im not sure about that but the atmosphere in general was 10/10. And its free entry before 12am!! Wonderful food and atmosphere. Live music like no other venue, I used to live Spanish Harlem and the neighborhood. Great vibes and cocktails, I really like that they served a variety of dishes for the range of price. I enjoyed the fish and chips, coleslaw was great too. 'Talk ya Talk' happening up in Harlem on a Tuesday night only a $10 cover!! They have rare artistic designs featuring African heritage and showcasing good artists for open mic. I will be back to sign some bars another day. Definitely a place to have some drinks and enjoy good time. Great staff, very friendly. Hole in the wall music venue with a solid food menu. Ordered a veggie burger and nachos. Prices were kinda high for the kind of place but food was good. Music was great.

name: Shu Jiao Fu Zhou
description: Simple Chinese cafe specializing in soups & noodles with a view of workers making dumplings by hand.
formatted_address: 295 Grand St, New York, NY 10002, USA
matched_google_address: 295 Grand St, New York, NY 10002, USA
reviews_text_combined: When we walked in, there was a long line, but it moved quickly. I only waited about 57 minutes to order. They have signs on the wall saying you should know your order before stepping up and clean up after yourself, but I noticed a lot of people dont really pay attention to that so just be prepared. What I ordered: Wheat Noodles with Peanut Sauce (#5) It was good, but I wish the sauce was thicker and more buttery so it would coat the noodles better. It was a bit watery, so the sauce kept sliding off when I picked up the noodles. Adding the chili oil from the table definitely helped improve the flavor. Small Pork-Filled Fish Ball Soup (#11) Pretty good overall. The broth was clean and hot (though it cooled down quickly). The fish balls tasted like real fish paste, not the powdery hotpot-style. Overall, it was a good experience two dishes for under $10 is hard to beat. Service was quick and efficient as long as youre ready to order without hesitation. Also, its cash only. We got our food to go, but would have sat inside if we didnt need to be someplace shortly after our stop in. We got the wheat noodles with peanut butter sauce and the pork and chive dumplings and with tip it was $10 for two people for lunch! What a bargain. The noodles were delightful and we added the leftover dumpling sauce, had we ate there I would have added Sriracha to it and it would have been perfect. The dumplings were crisp and steaming hot and delicious, though they did short us one so we only got seven instead of the eight we ordered. Make sure you count before you leave! We will be back and dine in to enhance our experience. We tried to go here a couple of weeks ago, but the line was long and we were very hungry, so we left. Good news was that today the line was short, but ended up pretty long by the time we left. It seemed to move quickly though. They also have plenty of tables, so by the time you order one is usually available before your food is ready. Given the lines, I was expecting the food to be better. We got a #1a and a #11. The noodles and meat in 1a were ok, but not great. The broth wasnt super flavorful. Im not sure if its what we ordered, but this was probably the first place from the infatuation best noodles in nyc place where we said we dont need to come back. Note, I love spicy food. Like really, really spicy. They didnt really have anything spicy, minus the chili oil, which isnt even spicy. It also was one of the most unappetizing things I have seen in a while. I am not sure how long it was been there, but I think it has more nastiness in it than bob Marleys hair. I am not sure what the red sauce is, but it isnt sriracha. Absolutely delicious food and quick service. BRING CASH! Its very busy inside but its common for you to ask to sit at a table with other people if seats are open. I got the wheat noodles with peanut butter sauce and vegetable dumplings for $8.25! Wow such a steal. Im vegetarian and with my two dishes I left full and satisfied. My friend recommended the wheat noodles vs the thick rice noodles because she thinks they soak up the sauce more and I was happy with them! Im sure personal preference factors into preferred noodle type. No frills place, but cant beat the price or the flavor here. I think its worth the hype I came here for lunch with a few friends who have never tried Fuzhounese food before. We ordered fried dumplings, steamed dumplings, peanut butter noodles, wonton soup, and fish ball soup. We all enjoyed the food and left super full. The prices are very affordable, we only spent $35 for a total of 4 people. There are a good amount of tables and also a bathroom for customers. I definitely will be coming back here soon!

name: Shuka
description: Place for contemporary Eastern Mediterranean food, wine, and cocktails in a stylish setting.
formatted_address: 38 MacDougal St, New York, NY 10012, USA
matched_google_address: 38 MacDougal St, New York, NY 10012, USA
reviews_text_combined: Amazing food and staff! Very generous portions for a very reasonable price. It's really worth it! The atmosphere is very nice and pleasant. Bathrooms are located downstairs (for accessibility). Good food. loved our server, very attentive. Loved the vibe we truly enjoyed the Moroccan mint tea, the fattoush salad, the zatar fries was so good. chicken kabab was flavorful the pistachio feta blend was so good w pita bread. Everything fresh. Reservation a must. it got crowded for a Monday night. Never thought of having an olive oil cake we tried it and loved it. We got to meet the executive chef Ayesha which was nice. The brunch menu was absolutely delicious with so much variety that it was hard to choose. Thankfully, our fantastic server was incredibly knowledgeable and helped guide us to some truly delightful selections. There were great options available for all dietary lifestyles, which was much appreciated. The staff was friendly, the space was beautifully decorated and festive, and the overall experience was wonderful. Highly recommend. Well definitely be back! Absolutely outstanding experience! The food at Shuka was excellent every dish was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. The service was warm and attentive, and the atmosphere had a great energy without being too loud. Everything from the appetizers to the desserts was spot on. Highly recommend this place if youre in SoHo and want a meal thats both delicious and beautifully presented. Ill definitely be coming back! The cocktails were creative and perfectly balanced, and the portions were just right. You can really taste the quality of the ingredients in every bite. Its rare to find a restaurant where everything food, service, and vibe comes together so perfectly. I ordered the malawach and as someone Yemenite, I can honestly saythey nailed it. Perfect texture, perfect flavor, just the real deal. We also had the fattoush salad, which wasnt the traditional version youd expect, but a fresher, more modern takeand it worked! There was a delay with our order, so they sent us a complimentary muesli with pears, and it was honestly the best Ive ever tasted. Everything was excellent, full of flavor, and had a great kick. Highly recommended!

name: Shukette
description: Bustling destination with chef's tables, turning out kebabs and seafood specialties.
formatted_address: 230 9th Ave, New York, NY 10001, USA
matched_google_address: 230 9th Ave, New York, NY 10001, USA
reviews_text_combined: The food and service were ! We celebrated our anniversary at Shukette and had such a memorable experience. From the moment we arrived, the energy in the space was vibrant and welcoming. We were seated at the chefs counter in the middle of the open kitchen, which made the night feel even more special. Watching the chefs prepare and plate the dishes added an interactive, immersive element that we truly enjoyed. The food was flavorful, fresh, and beautifully presented. Every dish felt thoughtfully prepared, and you can really taste the quality of the ingredients. The atmosphere is lively and the playlist was everything! What truly stood out was how the team acknowledged our anniversary. They surprised us with complimentary sides and desserts, which was such a generous and thoughtful gesture. It made the evening feel personalized and celebratory without being overdone. Overall, Shukette delivered not just a great meal, but a full experience. We left feeling celebrated and well taken care of. Highly recommend for a special occasion or a fun, energetic night out in NYC. Highly recommend! I am a huge fan of Chef Ayesha, so we went for my birthday and had the best time and the best meal! We were warmly greeted by the hostess and the restaurant manager (who happened to be at the host stand - very professional!). We had a great booth right next to the window, but I wish we had sat at the bar so I could watch Chef in action (next time!). The meal was delicious, perfect for family-style sharing and noshing! Everything was fantastic, perfectly prepared, and beautifully presented. The flavors are so cool and unique, but also familiar and comforting. Frena completely won me over from the very first bite. Soft and fluffy on top with a crispy, almost scorched bottom, the texture was incredibly satisfying. The seasoning looks simple, yet somehow it is deeply addictive. The only downside is that it is way too small and disappeared in seconds. I paired it with the labneh dip, which was also excellent. The chili powder sprinkled on top is the true star. It cuts through the richness perfectly and keeps everything from feeling heavy. The presentation is beautiful as well and instantly brightens your mood. The biggest surprise for me was the grilled fish. It carries a subtle Thai inspired touch, with wonderful seasoning and absolutely no fishy taste at all. The dipping sauce is quite spicy but pairs beautifully with the fish and brings everything together. This was a meal that left me wanting more and I will definitely be back. The place is very popular, so it is best to arrive right around peak dining time or you will most likely have to wait in line. Every bite = wow. Outstanding is an understatement. The Labneh, frena, ROASTED CAULIFLOWER, joojeh chicken were all amazingly good. The fish in a cage has a great presentation, its good, dont get me wrong, its just Ive had better whole fish. We also love the mic drop aka tahini soft serve, very interesting! And yummm. Our server Oriella is wonderful, as well as other staffs, they were all super friendly and lovely. I absolutely adore the experience and would totally come back to try more dishes. We ate at Shukette in Chelsea and even though we were seated in the outdoor sectionwhich wasnt the most comfortablethe food and service completely made up for it. The standouts were incredible: Shakshuka Braised Heirloom Butter Beans rich, flavorful, and totally unique. Grass-Fed Shish Kebab perfectly seasoned and cooked. Not Your Average Hummus and the Beet Tzatziki fun, delicious, and definitely not average! The staff was warm and on point, and the energy of the place is great. The indoor dining room looked amazing, but they were fully booked, so well definitely try again for indoor seating next time. Great location near the subway, delicious dishes across the board, and genuinely fantastic service. Highly recommend Shukette!

name: Sisters

formatted_address: 900 Fulton St, Brooklyn, NY 11238, USA

reviews_text_combined: What a cute place for brunch! The space has a ton of natural light and the wood and greenery makes you feel warm and cozy! The food is absolutely delicious. Bravo on the menu as everything we tried it was so good. The mimosas are good as is the Bloody Mary. This is a great place for a couple or a small group. There is lots of seating but often busy so Id suggest making a reservation. Gorgeous and popular brunch spot. They get super busy and strict 90-min table seating. Food was amazing as was service. Had the shrimp and grits, French toast, and fried chicken sandwich. Would come back. Delicious fish sandwich, perfectly crisp sweet potato fries, and a great cauliflower starterplus lots of dishes with labneh, which I loved. We walked in as a party of two on a Tuesday night and grabbed seats at the bar for dinner, which ended up being the perfect spot. The staff was friendly, the ambiance was warm and lively, and the whole place just had a great vibe. Worth noting: there are a few small steps up into the restaurant, but once youre inside, it feels cozy and welcoming. Would definitely come back! Food and Ambiance was really good!!! A bit loud to have a conversation but its all worth it. Their drinks were also amazing as well. We got their Chicken wings for appetizers and id say go order them! This was hands down the best macaroni and cheese Ive ever had and I consider myself a connoisseur. Its an alfredo style mac and cheese, very filling (and heavy on the dairy for my fellow lactose intolerant folks), and so good that I had to order for a second time. Their other food is awesome too, but the mac and cheese was a real showstopper. The salmon was excellent, really enjoyed the sauce over it and quite well cooked.

name: Smoke Jazz & Supper Club
description: A cozy venue with clean sight lines & good sound featuring jazz, blues & Latin acts, plus eats.
formatted_address: 2751 Broadway, New York, NY 10025, USA
matched_google_address: 2751 Broadway, New York, NY 10025, USA
reviews_text_combined: Was a nice cozy and warm, welcoming was fantastic. Seating was quick. Food was excellent. However, table are very tiny seating are very tight. Music was nice was expecting more. No one singing just playing on instrument player was professional not up to my test. Lets start with the good part: the atmosphere is incredible. Red velvet, candlelight, and live jazz so close you can feel the bass through your glass. The cocktails are top-tier, and the food easily outperforms what most music venues even attempt. My crab cakes were crisp perfection, the salad was fresh, and the service stayed calm even with a packed house. Now for the part that ruined the vibe. The mandatory entre rule. I came for drinks and small plates, happily spent several times the cost of an entre, and was still told I had to order one or leave it uneaten. It felt like a cheap money grab at a place that otherwise screams class. Nothing kills the mood faster than being told your perfectly good dinner order isnt enough. I still respect the music and the kitchen, both are excellent, but that policy left a bad taste I cant ignore. Three stars. Great night, unnecessary greed. The new Smoke is a massive upgrade. The venue is much larger now, which means the bar has leveled upthe cocktails are easily some of the best in the city. The kitchen is still undefeated; the food is as awesome as ever. We saw Theo Croker, and while it was a modern, stripped-back set, the band was incredibly sharp. The Music: A very cool, modern jazz feel. The Twist: Even without a DJ or heavy electronics, Theo and the band held it down. The Talent: Theos trumpet work was top-tier, and the ensemble playing was seamless. A great night of music that actually fills the bigger room perfectly. Between the great kitchen and the martinis, its still one of the best dinner show in town. There's no other Jazz Club like Smoke. I've been going there for years before the renovation. It maintains it's status of elegance, great management, good food and service. I go to many jazz clubs that honestly aren't excellent in all these categories. The musicians are some of the greats. Thank you for maintaing your excellan e. We went to Smoke Jazz & Supper Club last night and had an incredible time. The show was absolutely amazing, especially the Monk tribute, which was unbelievable. Both the food and the performance exceeded our expectations. We had the octopus, NY strip, and coq au vin, and everything was cooked perfectly. Such a great experience all around.

name: Sofreh

reviews_text_combined: Absolutely delicious food and its own mix on a variety of Persian cuisine. Service was great with thoughtful staff. Only note I would make is that we were 5people for a 6person reservation and they charged us 20$ ( yes I agreed to it when I made reso but this change is completely normal for dinner). I told them immediately when we arrived. This is despite the table really only being for 5 people to begin with. 5 stars for everything but management. I would give it 2 stars. The extra star is for decor music and design, last for service which was great. I would recommend the lamb shank and steak. Egg plant was delicious and a variety of very tasty drinks. I would recommend this spot, but for a second time visit for me, this far in BK and poor management where it took almost 90 mins for our meal to be served...I'm likely not to go back. Worth the trip but only once Absolutely loved Sofreh. Every dish was full of flavor and beautifully presented. The atmosphere feels calm, elegant, and truly special. The staff were kind and attentive, and the whole experience had that warm Persian charm that makes you want to come back. Definitely one of the best restaurants in New York City. I dont say this lightly: Sofreh is truly one of the citys standout restaurants. Every dish reflects a level of mastery thats hard to overstate. The kitchen takes authentic Persian flavors and elevates them with real creativitythe ribeye kebab is a perfect example. I was equally taken with the eggplant and whey dip; it was so good that I bought their cookbook afterward just to understand the sorcery behind it. And a special shoutout to the wonderful service team, who made the experience even better. I absolutely loved the atmosphere at Sofreh. The space is so beautiful and thoughtfully designed, and even the staff uniforms added to how stylish and put together everything felt. The whole place has such a cute and welcoming vibe, and the staff were beyond nice. The food was fantastic. The dishes had small creative twists while still staying very authentic to traditional Persian flavors. The tahdig is an absolute must. It was the best I have ever had in my life. Just note that it is limited to one order per table. Portions are not very sharable unless you order several different dishes, which is what we ended up doing. I also tried the kabob, which was delicious, especially with the pomegranate sauce. That said, it was more like a steak than a traditional kabob, so just something to keep in mind. Sadly, I could not get a picture of it because it was so good we ate it right away. Overall, Sofreh is a beautiful restaurant with amazing food, wonderful staff, and a great atmosphere. I would definitely come back and recommend it to anyone looking for an elevated Persian dining experience in New York. Insatiable food. The flavors of each dish were unique to its own but combined and complimented each other so beautifully. Service was great - everyone was so kind. They were also well knowledgeable on the dishes and described the drinks and food with such detail! The suggestions were amazing. Unfortunately didnt take any pics but heres the dessert. I was so full but I couldnt stop eating the ice cream. 10/10 through and through.

name: Soothr
description: Intimate space with al fresco dining, offering Thai noodles and soups, plus cocktails.
formatted_address: 204 E 13th St, New York, NY 10003, USA
matched_google_address: 204 E 13th St, New York, NY 10003, USA
reviews_text_combined: Our dinner was absolutely spectacular! Our server, Nina was warm, attentive, and offered excellent recommendations that truly elevated the entire experience. It was a great dining experience thanks to her! We started with the Lobster and Ha Gao from the Lunar New Year menu. The lobster was incredibly delicious which perfectly cooked and generously coated in a spicy sauce that complemented the sweetness of the meat beautifully. The Ha Gao were delicate and flavorful, with a satisfying bite. The Hoo Johr was also a standout! It was crispy on the outside, savory on the inside, and perfect as an appetizer to share. For our mains, the Panang Duck Lychee Curry was hands down the best rich, creamy, and packed with bold flavors, with just the right balance of sweetness from the lychee. The Shrimp Pad Thai was equally impressive, featuring plump shrimp, well-balanced seasoning, and perfectly cooked noodles. We ended the meal with mango sticky rice. Even though mango isnt in season, the dessert was absolutely amazing! The sticky rice was warm and fragrant, paired with sweet coconut milk, mango Ice cream and fresh mango.. it was the perfect ending to a fantastic dinner. Highly recommend! This restaurant really blow my mind and reset my taste for thai food. I have always like it. Been on several restaurants. But none like Soothr. Food was amazing. Unique experience with each bite. Service was fantastic, and staff supper friendly . Restaurant overall is everything you need if u looking for great food. We start with some crispy Chiken, fried dumplings 10/10 pad thai , duck in curry sauce, wow those too were also fantastic. Can't wait to go back . Dessert was sweet and delicious... While im writing this im having a smile in my face,enjoying my food again Had a great experience at Soothr today! Jesse provided excellent service, she was very attentive, friendly, and made us feel very welcome throughout the meal. The crab roe curry noodles (Karee Pu Noodles) were absolutely delicious, rich in flavor and perfectly balanced. The food came out quickly, and the overall service was efficient and very nice. Definitely would come back again! I was in town for the weekend and couldn't wait to co to Soothr because of their reputation. I was starting to get packed and it was tight. It was like a Thai-Chinatown (Yaowarat) vibe. We were given an hour and a half time limit which is normal but I feel like we were being rushed. Started off with drinks, I wasn't a fan of my cocktail. Duck egg rolls had more wood ear mushrooms than duck. I was excited about Karee Pu (crab curry) the texture of the noodles were great. The curry was extra thick and had a strong cumin taste to it, we weren't a fan. We also got the duck noodles, once again the noodles were great. The duck was cooked perfectly but the gravy was so sweet and that was it, sweet. I love Thai food because of the mix of flavors, sweet, spicy, sour, salty, but this place didn't do it for us. I really wanted to like it but we were pretty underwhelmed. Jane was our server and did an amazing job explaining all the dishes and options! We went for the Valentine's special and the crab croquett and Yaowarat Pad Mii were delicious. Would come back and try some more next time! Thanks Jane for making our Valentine's memorable

name: Spring Shabu-Shabu
description: Stylish Japanese outfit preparing meats, seafood, noodles, and veggies in tableside hot pots.

reviews_text_combined: We had a great experience last night at Spring Shabu-Shabu. you have to come in and check in because there is usually a wait, we waited around 30 minutes to get to our table. Kelly our server did a great job helping us choose all the selection available to eat. I picked vegetable broth, and my husband did kimchi broth. You can pick from all vegetables to meats from mushrooms to noodles. There is a great variety to pick from to make your Shabu-Shabu experience feel at home. We are definitely returning for more thank you Spring Shabu-Shabu for making this night a great night for us. FIRST TIME HERE BUT NOT THE LAST I WILL BE BACK!!!! Food was amazing had many varieties to choose from and they were all good, the facility was clean, and comfortable the environment was perfect I was able to have conversations with my friends without having to scream at the top of my lungs the best part of this experience was our waitress Kelly M, if you get Kelly M as your waitress she will take care of you I promise you youll be lucky. She is friendly and pleasant I told her it was my first time at this restaurant and first time having a hot pot experience. She took time to help give me recommendations and insight on the menu because my friend and I have dietary restrictions she was attentive to us always checked up on us, when we were running low on drinks she would realize and without having to ask she would make sure our cups were full. Kelly M was friendly she would talk with us make jokes and she made it feel like a welcoming environment like she was happy to be there, help us, and take time to talk to us too. I can whole heartedly say Kelly M as our waitress for the first time at this restaurant made the experience better we were lucky to have her as our waitress and when we go back we hope to have her as our waitress again cause shes the best. Ive tried a lot of hotpot places, but this one is truly my number one. Every time I come here, the food is super fresh, the service is fast, and the whole vibe just feels cozy and welcoming. Their mala soup is honestly unmatched the perfect balance of spicy, flavorful, and comforting. Its the kind of soup that keeps you coming back because nothing else tastes the same! The ingredients are always high quality, and everything cooks beautifully in the broth. Ive brought friends here and they end up loving it just as much as I do. The staff is always kind and helpful, especially if youre unsure what to try. And I HAVE to mention the vanilla + green tea swirl ice cream at the end its my favorite treat! Its creamy, refreshing, and the perfect finish after a warm hotpot meal. Overall, this place never disappoints. Amazing food, great service, clean environment, and such good value for the quality. Highly, highly recommend to anyone who loves hotpot or wants to try it for the first time! Get the spicy broth. Its delicious. Even if you dont add meat, there is plenty of protein to add. I recommend the lamb if you do. The crabs arent soft shell. The egg needs to be broken first. The whole party needs to be there to be seated. Everyone gets their own broth regardless if the size of your party. This is a great Shabu place : clean , fast service and great food quality. Its a bit noisy but you can still have conversations without shouting. Id definitely go again and the ice cream was definitely a great thing to finish off such a great meal Just a side note that the building has two entrances , the one with the stairs is the easiest but if you end up like me in the rear door , no panic, you just need to walk a bit and take the elevator and you will end there

name: St. Mazie Bar & Supper Club

If you love a place with personality and an atmosphere that makes the experience feel a little special, St. Mazie is definitely worth it Follow me me on TikTok for more @outwithshea IG: @sheanadagrin Beautiful ambience. Finally found the jazz spot we were searching for!!! Incredible music and honestly delicious dinner. Usually its one or the other but this place delivered! As soon as you walk into Taqueria Milear, you immediately feel the warmth and the cozy, inviting vibe. Im genuinely in love with this place everything tastes homemade because it truly is. They have amazing weekly specials, too: Happy Hour on Mondays ALL DAY with classic margaritas for only $10 (so good!) Tecate Tuesday, perfect if you love an easy, chilled beer And my absolute favorite: the Carnitas Combo tender, flavorful pulled pork that hits every time, available any day of the week. If youre vegetarian or avoid dairy, the Vegetable Burrito is incredible. Its hearty, packed with flavor, and wrapped in their house-made corn tortilla, which makes all the difference. Dont skip the chips it comes with a scoop of guacamole on top of the warm chips. Heaven. Brunch here is another level: Their chilaquiles are comforting and perfectly seasoned And the eggs with steak is such a satisfying combo The cocktails are unreal especially their signature pineapple spicy cocktail. Truly one of the best Ive had. Overall, Taqueria Milear is one of those rare spots where everything feels thoughtful, homemade, and delicious. I cant recommend it enough! First time I came here was like a year ago, and Ive always meant to make a review about Milear; so here we go : First of all , Manuel and Ryan provided wonderful service, always anticipating your need before you even asked, once I didnt know what to order ; Therefore being opened to suggestions; Manuel Suggested the carnitas , and since then Ive been in love with it and been coming back for more ; in addition to it , their consistency its unbelievable top notch food. Come try it out for yourself. Bottom line theyve succeeded at making carnitas an Unforgettable Delicious experience. Immediately upon entering the service was so warm and kind. Roberto was so welcoming and you could tell that he was so proud of the food and culture here. The owner even came and said hi to us at one point! And they have a LOT to be proud of. The homemade tortillas were unlike anything Ive had before. Im usually not a fan of carnitas, but the ones they make here are incredible fresh and delicious. If youre not hungry, definitely come for the drinks. The spicy chipotle margarita is a whole meal in a drink - SO good. I was only able to get a picture of the drinks but look how beautiful they are! They also have a great happy hour deal where margaritas are $9-10. Such a great hidden gem in crown heights. Highly recommend for atmosphere, drinks, food and service. Fantastic place: the carnitas is so good, the margaritas are great too, made with agave syrup, great chips and guac, specials and happy hours (9$ margs during the week). Just a great spot.

name: Taqueria Ramirez
